message:
style+fix: clippy lint fixes, explicit imports, and Rust edition compatibility

Clippy lint fixes across messaging, database, lab, net, and runtime:
- Remove unnecessary .clone() calls on values that are not used after
  the move (consumer, control, fabric, session/conformance, contract,
  obligation, projection, mysql)
- Inline format arguments: format!("...{}", x) → format!("...{x}")
  (capability/tokens)
- Use method reference instead of closure: |r| r.name() → T::name
  (federation)
- Replace "".to_owned() with String::new() (service)
- Simplify bool-to-usize: if/else 0/1 → usize::from(!x.is_empty())
  (fabric source_symbol_count)

Explicit imports in database/transaction.rs:
- Replace `use super::*` glob imports in pg and mysql sub-modules with
  explicit import lists to improve readability and avoid namespace
  pollution

Compiler compatibility fix in net/quic_native/forensic_log.rs:
- Split `if let ... && guard` let-chain into nested if/let for broader
  Rust edition compatibility

Raw pointer modernization in runtime/reactor/io_uring.rs:
- Replace `&mut counter as *mut u64` with `&raw mut counter` (Rust
  2024 edition raw reference syntax, avoids creating intermediate
  mutable reference)

Event recorder fix in lab/oracle/fabric.rs:
- Add missing .clone() on Arc<Mutex<Vec>> so the events handle is
  retained for post-report assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
